Whether that be mentally, physically, as I've been a physical, a rehab tech for working in physical therapy, as well as I,
 Occupational therapy. So I just love enjoying seeing people being a better position and they were the previous day. I look forward to helping the kids and helping make sure they allow me to remember everything that I was also taught.

I have actually dealt with a situation similar to one of my coaching gigs. I was coaching on all all sport team where we basically did multi-sports on different days and there was a young girl who her attitude would kind of be iffy on some days. She'll be good. Some days she won't be kind of just wake up and have to see what it is with her, but she was she was a good kid. She just wanted things to go her way and so there was a we were playing a game.
 That had to do with hula hoops. And she wanted a specific color but another child, she ended up picking that color before her. And so she got an attitude, she threw a tantrum, she kind of went up to the girl and everything. And so I just corrected, the behavior, just reassuring her, that, you know, we are limited with our equipment that we have and that we could take turns. And that, you know, sometimes we might not always get what we want and it's okay, we leave.
 Have to wait or were you have to settle for something else? But I also took her to the side and just talk to her and see if she was, you know, how hard day and everything was going because, you know, family things can kind of affect attitudes as well. But we kind of got that figured out the other young girl, she
 Was open to just giving the girl, her hula hoop of another color and she was okay with just picking another one. So we kind of work that one out.
